description: In January 2016, the Bank of Japan, led by Haruhiko Kuroda, surprised global markets by adopting a negative interest rate policy (-0.1%) to combat persistent deflation. The scenario captures the Japanese public and domestic banking sector's skepticism regarding the efficacy of unconventional monetary policy.

key_events.description: Bank of Japan surprises markets with the 'negative rate' announcement.
Major Japanese commercial banks report squeezed profit margins, fueling public opposition.
Economic data shows inflation targets remain unmet, deepening skepticism.
The policy is deemed a failure by a large portion of the public as cash hoarding persists.

calibration_notes: Useful for modeling reactions to 'unconventional' monetary policy that creates immediate friction with the banking sector.
